commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=4b21ca26f33b29577f8eb71818e3909dffec2704)
commands.review_summary:
The PR removes `encoding="UTF-8"` from the Windows Scheduled Task XML declaration generated by `buildScheduledTaskXml()`.

Reproducibility: no. live reproduction was performed here. The path is source-reproducible: current main emits a UTF-8 declaration, writes `daemon-task.xml` as UTF-8, and passes it to `schtasks /Create /XML`; the linked report supplies the localized Windows error.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor real-behavior proof plus a fix for the focused test and encoding compatibility before normal merge review can proceed.

target.title: fix(daemon): remove encoding declaration from Windows task XML
target.body:
fixes #315 
On Windows systems with ANSI codepage (e.g. GBK/CP936 for Chinese locale), schtasks.exe fails to import the scheduled task XML when it contains `encoding="UTF-8"` but the file is saved as UTF-8 without BOM.

Error observed:
  ERROR: The task XML is malformed.
  (1,40)::错误: 无法切换编码

The XML content contains only ASCII characters (paths, user names, booleans), so the `encoding` attribute is not required per XML spec. Removing it allows schtasks to parse the file using its default ANSI codepage without attempting a codepage switch, which avoids the error on all Windows locales.

Tested on Windows 11 (Chinese) – after this change, `summarize daemon install` completes successfully and the task is registered.
target.author: zevan770

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
